questions.answer: Controlling is the process of ensuring that the activities of an organization are in line with its objectives. It involves comparing actual performance with the standard performance and taking corrective action if there is a deviation.
key_points: Controlling is a part of the management process

questions.q: What is the main objective of controlling?
questions.answer: The main objective of controlling is to ensure that the activities of an organization are in line with its objectives and to achieve the desired results.
key_points: Controlling helps in achieving organizational goals

questions.answer: Planning sets the goals and objectives of an organization, while controlling ensures that the activities of an organization are in line with its objectives.
key_points: Planning sets goals, controlling ensures achievement

questions.answer: The functions of controlling are to compare actual performance with standard performance, to take corrective action, and to ensure that the activities of an organization are in line with its objectives.
key_points: Functions of controlling include comparison and corrective action

questions.answer: A standard is a yardstick against which the actual performance of an organization is compared to determine the level of achievement.
key_points: Standard is a yardstick for comparison

questions.answer: A standard cost is the cost of a product or service that an organization aims to achieve, based on the standard inputs and outputs.
key_points: Standard cost is the target cost

questions.q: What are the benefits of standard costing?
questions.answer: The benefits of standard costing include improved cost control, increased efficiency, and better decision-making. It also helps in identifying areas of inefficiency and taking corrective action.
key_points: Benefits of standard costing include improved cost control and efficiency

questions.answer: A variance is the difference between the actual cost or actual performance and the standard cost or standard performance. It can be either favorable (F) or unfavorable (U).
key_points: Variance is the difference between actual and standard performance

questions.q: Distinguish between favorable variance and unfavorable variance.
questions.answer: A favorable variance occurs when the actual cost or actual performance is less than the standard cost or standard performance. An unfavorable variance occurs when the actual cost or actual performance is more than the standard cost or standard performance.
key_points: Favorable variance is less than standard, unfavorable variance is more than standard

questions.answer: A budget is a detailed plan of financial and non-financial resources required to achieve the objectives of an organization within a specific period of time.
key_points: Budget is a plan for resource allocation

questions.answer: The types of budgets include master budget, operating budget, capital budget, and cash budget.
key_points: Types of budgets include master budget and operating budget

questions.q: Explain the concept of zero-based budgeting.
questions.answer: Zero-based budgeting is a method of budgeting where every expense is justified and approved before it is included in the budget. It starts from a zero base and allocates funds for each expense based on its justification.
key_points: Zero-based budgeting involves justification of every expense

questions.q: Distinguish between static budget and flexible budget.
questions.answer: A static budget is a budget that remains constant even if there are changes in the level of activity. A flexible budget is a budget that adjusts to changes in the level of activity.
key_points: Static budget remains constant, flexible budget adjusts to changes

questions.q: Explain the concept of management by exception.
questions.answer: Management by exception is a control technique where only exceptional situations are brought to the notice of the management, and they take corrective action. It involves comparing actual performance with standard performance and taking action only when there is a deviation.
key_points: Management by exception involves corrective action for deviations

questions.q: State the advantages of management by exception.
questions.answer: The advantages of management by exception include efficient use of time, reduced paperwork, and improved decision-making. It also helps in focusing on critical areas where corrective action is needed.
key_points: Advantages of management by exception include efficient use of time and decision-making
